summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform clipboard hijacker with persistence
details: Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` impersonates the popular `@juggle/resize-observer` and the README is copy-pasted from that legitimate package, but the shipped code is unrelated to ResizeObserver. The `npm-install.cjs` postinstall script downloads `https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py` to `/tmp`, executes it via `python3`, and then `pip install --break-system-packages` a bundled Python package (`clipboard_guardian/`) which is a clipboard-hijacking clipper. On Linux the installer escalates privileges via `sudo -n` and `pkexec` to install `xclip`/`wl-clipboard` and uses apt-get/pacman/dnf as needed. The clipper daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) polls the clipboard, detects crypto addresses across 40+ chains (BTC, ETH, SOL, XMR, TRON, etc.), and silently rewrites them to attacker-controlled wallets seeded into a config file at install (e.g. ETH `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`). The daemon installs cross-platform autostart persistence — Linux systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` (with `loginctl enable-linger` to survive logout), macOS LaunchAgent labelled `com.apple.python.runtime`, and Windows HKCU Run value `PyRuntimeBroker` — and uses `setproctitle` to disguise its process name to match these legit-sounding identifiers. The daemon also checks for running task managers (`taskmgr.exe`, `htop`, `gnome-system-monitor`) for evasion. Any installer copying a cryptocurrency address after this package is installed will have funds redirected to the attacker's wallets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
